label: Telegram Web — Messenger Browser
description: Telegram WebK (React/TS). QR-first login or phone → SMS code. Left sidebar: chat list. Right pane: message history + composer. MTProto over WebSocket. Russian locale common. No infinite scroll — paginated history via scroll-to-top trigger. PHONE-NATIVE: no bypass for registration — requires real phone number and SMS code. No OAuth/email-only path. From session analysis: 54 mentions across agents.
source_patterns: Login: QR code shown by default → scan with Telegram app → auto-auth
Alt login: 'Log in by phone number' → enter phone → receive code in app → enter code
Chat list scroll: vertical list, click to select, active chat highlighted
Message history: scroll up to load older messages (pagination at top)
Send message: type in composer (bottom), press Enter or click send button
Media: click attachment icon → file/photo selector → send with optional caption
Reaction: long-click message → reaction picker → select emoji

active_hours.description: Always-on messenger — steady usage across day, dips late night

scroll.description: Chat list: smooth scroll, short passes. History: page-up at top to load older messages

login.note: PHONE-NATIVE. No email-only registration. QR login requires Telegram app on the same phone. SMS code login requires a real SIM. No bypass or workaround available.

platform_specific.sequence_hint: navigate web.telegram.org → QR login or phone+code → wait for sidebar to populate → click chat → scroll history → read messages → type in composer → send → optionally attach media
